pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 17 and export_result.sh line 9 - filename is consistently used as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/rc_beam_elevation.dxf

Task description and verifier.py line 80 confirms 5000mm ± 100mm tolerance. 5000mm (5-meter) span is standard for reinforced concrete beams. Web search confirmed typical beam depths are 60-65mm per meter of span, making 5m spans typical in structural engineering.

Task description and verifier.py line 80 confirms 600mm ± 100mm tolerance. Web search confirmed 600mm deep beams are commonly used in structural engineering practice, typically for spans of 9-10 meters or for heavier load cases.

Task description line 5 explicitly states 'Use 40mm clear cover (lines at Y=40 and Y=560)'. Web search confirmed 40mm clear cover falls within typical practical ranges for concrete structures. This value correctly produces rebar spacing of 520mm (600-40-40), which matches verifier tolerance of 500-540mm (line 99).

Task description line 5 states 'repeat every 200mm along the beam length'. Calculation: (5000-100)/200 = 24.5 ≈ 25 stirrups matches 'approx 25 stirrups' in description. Web search confirmed 200mm spacing is standard for residential reinforced concrete applications and falls within typical 150-200mm range. Verifier expects 190-210mm median spacing (line 113).

ACI (AutoCAD Color Index) standard: Index 8 = Dark Gray (RGB: 65,65,65). Verified via web search of ezdxf documentation and gohtx.com color reference. Task description line 5 confirms 'Color: Gray/8'.
ACI standard: Index 1 = Red (RGB: 255,0,0). Verified via web search of ACI color systems and AutoCAD documentation. Task description line 5 confirms 'Color: Red/1'.

ACI standard: Index 3 = Green (RGB from standard palette). Verified via web search of ACI color systems. Task description line 5 confirms 'Color: Green/3'. Green is among the first 7 named ACI colors (1=red, 2=yellow, 3=green, 4=cyan, 5=blue, 6=magenta, 7=white).

privileged_info_summary: This task specifies creating a reinforced concrete beam elevation drawing in LibreCAD with verified dimensions and standards. The beam is 5000mm (5 meters) long by 600mm deep, representing a standard structural element. Reinforcement includes two horizontal main reinforcement bars at 40mm clear cover (positioned at Y=40 and Y=560), which produces a center-to-center spacing of 520mm. Vertical stirrups are spaced at 200mm intervals starting at X=100, producing approximately 25 stirrups across the beam length—all within standard engineering practice. Layer assignments use standard ACI (AutoCAD Color Index) codes: CONCRETE layer (ACI 8 = dark gray), REBAR_MAIN layer (ACI 1 = red), and REBAR_STIRRUPS layer (ACI 3 = green). The output file is saved as rc_beam_elevation.dxf. All numerical claims in metadata are verified against engineering standards and structural design practice.
